DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT NTC OF GENERAL DEMURRER; REQ. FOR JUDICIAL NTC; APPENDIX OF FOREIGN AUTHORITIES AND POS FILED BY DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION HEARING SET FOR JUL-27-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
REPLY IN SUPPORT OF SPECIAL AND GENERAL DEMURRERS TO PLTF RAHMON MOMOH'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, FIFTH, SIXTH, AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, FIFTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE CIRCUMSTANCES THAT SUPPORT THE INFERENCE THAT DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS WERE BASED ON RACE, COLOR, OR NATIONAL ORIGIN DISCRIMINATION. PLAINTIFF MAY BE UNDER A MISAPPREHENSION OF THE PRIMA FACIE ELEMENTS: THE FOURTH ELEMENT IS NOT PRECISELY AS STATED IN THE OPPOSITION AT 2 (PERSON NOT IN PROTECTED CLASS SELECTED). "A PRIMA FACIE CASE GENERALLY MEANS THE PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE EVIDENCE THAT (1) THE PLAINTIFF WAS A MEMBER OF A PROTECTED CLASS, (2) THE PLAINTIFF WAS QUALIFIED FOR THE POSITION HE OR SHE SOUGHT OR WAS PERFORMING COMPETENTLY IN THE POSITION HELD, (3) THE PLAINTIFF SUFFERED AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION, SUCH AS TERMINATION, DEMOTION, OR DENIAL OF AN AVAILABLE JOB, AND (4) SOME OTHER CIRCUMSTANCE SUGGESTS A DISCRIMINATORY MOTIVE." SCOTCH V. ART INSTITUTE OF CALIFORNIA, 173 CAL. APP. 4TH 986, 1004 (2009). AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND PER PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST IN HIS OPPOSITION PAPERS. THE DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED THAT PLAINTIFF WAS DISCRIMINATED AGAINST BASED ON HIS AGE AND THAT DEFENDANT'S PROMOTIONAL PRACTICES HAVE A DISPARATE IMPACT ON EMPLOYEES OVER FORTY YEARS OLD. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. THE PROPOSED PAPERS ARE IRRELEVANT TO THE PENDING MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR # 7489

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF RAHMON MOMOH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND FOR SANCTIONS - THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL GRANTED. NO LATER THAN MAY 11, 2013, DEFENDANT CPUC SHALL RESPOND TO PLAINTIFF'S SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES NOS. 4 AND 5, MODIFIED AS FOLLOWS: SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 4, "PLEASE SET FORTH IN SPREADSHEET FORM, FOR JOB CLASSIFICATIONS PURA-III, PURA-IV, AND PURA-V, THE PROMOTION HISTORY, BY RACE, OF CPUC EMPLOYEES IN EACH CPUC DIVISION, FROM JANUARY 1, 2008 THROUGH DECEMBER 31, 2011, SHOWING IDENTITY OF EMPLOYEE, RACE, DATES OF PROMOTION, DIVISION, AND CATEGORY." SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 5, "PLEASE SET FORTH IN SPREADSHEET FORM, FOR JOB CLASSIFICATIONS PURA-III, PURA-IV, AND PURA-V, THE PROMOTION HISTORY, BY AGE, OF CPUC EMPLOYEES IN EACH CPUC DIVISION, FROM JANUARY 1, 2008 THROUGH DECEMBER 31, 2011, SHOWING IDENTITY OF EMPLOYEE, AGE, DATES OF PROMOTION, DIVISION, AND CATEGORY." PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE A WRITTEN ORDER. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. JUDGE PRO TEM: ERNST HALPERIN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANGELA POURTABIB, CSR # 13714 (TEL. 866.299.5127)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatoriers And For Sanctions; Memo
Off Calendar Mar-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard M. Rogers, Esq. of the Law Office of Richard M. Rogers, Tel. 415.981.9788, for plaintiff and moving party;
Sande Van Der Heide, Esq. of Ruiz & Sparrow, LLP, Tel. 510.594.5643, for defendant.

PLAINTIFF RAHMON MOMOH's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And For Sanctions - The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court ruled as follows: Motion to compel GRANTED. No later than May 11, 2013, Defendant CPUC shall respond to Plaintiff's Specially Prepared Interrogatories Nos. 4 and 5, modified as follows: Special Interrogatory 4, "Please set forth in spreadsheet form, for job classifications PURA-III, PURA-IV, and PURA-V, the promotion history, by race, of CPUC employees in each CPUC Division, from January 1, 2008 through December 31, 2011, showing identity of employee, race, dates of promotion, Division, and category." Special Interrogatory 5, "Please set forth in spreadsheet form, for job classifications PURA-III, PURA-IV, and PURA-V, the promotion history, by age, of CPUC employees in each CPUC Division, from January 1, 2008 through December 31, 2011, showing identity of employee, age, dates of promotion, Division, and category." Plaintiff is to prepare a written order. Request for sanctions is DENIED.
Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR # 13714 (Tel. 866.299.5127)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-28-2012 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RICHARD ROGERS, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF RICHARD ROGERS, 415.981.9788, FOR PLAINTIFF RAHMON MOMOH.
SANDER VAN DER HEIDE, ESQ., RUIZ & SPEROW, 510.594.7980, FOR DEFNEDNAT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC UTILITIES COMMISSION.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Defendant waited until Plaintiff was ineligible for promotion to post the open position. This shows a circumstance that suggests a discriminatory motive. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-09-2012 Continued To Oct-24-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sander Van Der Heide, Esq. of Ruiz & Sperow, Tel. 510.594.5346, appearing for Defendant and moving party;
Richard M. Rogers, Esq. of the L/O of Richard Rogers, Tel. 415.981.9788, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part. The demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth, and ninth causes of action. As to the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege circumstances that support the inference that Defendant's actions were based on race, color, or national origin discrimination. Plaintiff may be under a misapprehension of the prima facie elements: The fourth element is not precisely as stated in the Opposition at 2 (person not in protected class selected). "A prima facie case generally means the plaintiff must provide evidence that (1) the plaintiff was a member of a protected class, (2) the plaintiff was qualified for the position he or she sought or was performing competently in the position held, (3) the plaintiff suffered an adverse employment action, such as termination, demotion, or denial of an available job, and (4) some other circumstance suggests a discriminatory motive." Scotch v. Art Institute of California, 173 Cal. App. 4th 986, 1004 (2009). As to the ninth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend per Plaintiff's request in his opposition papers. The demurrer is otherwise overruled. Plaintiff has adequately alleged that Plaintiff was discriminated against based on his age and that Defendant's promotional practices have a disparate impact on employees over forty years old. The request for judicial notice is denied. The proposed papers are irrelevant to the pending motion. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489
